MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-29-2018 Continued To Sep-06-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. WALTER J. SCOTT, ESQ. of Scott Law Group (Tel: 214-755-1978) appearing for the plaintiff;
Atty. KAREN K. STROMEYER, ESQ. of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney (Tel: 415-788-1900) appearing for defendants Andrea McGary and Daniel Iannitelli

DEFENDANTS ANDREA MCGARY AND DANIEL IANNITELLI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ON PLAINTIFF WOODSIDE PRIORITY, LLC'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

The matter is argued by the parties. The Court defers its ruling in part and confirms its tentative ruling in part. As to the breach of contract and as to the legal malpractice only, the Court continues today's hearing to September 6, 2018 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion. The Court permits plaintiff's counsel to submit by 5:00 p.m. on 8/31/18 an additional declaration by Mr. Garlock only as to scienter. The Court does not permit plaintiff's counsel to submit new argument. The Court permits defense counsel to submit by 5:00 p.m. on 9/5/18 a response brief on why scienter is not established. The Court indicates that it will not issue a further tentative ruling in advance of the 9/6/18 hearing. The Court otherwise confirms its tentative ruling to rule today as follows: Defendants Andrea McGary and Daniel Iannitelli's motion for summary judgment on plaintiff Woodside Priority, LLC's first amended complaint is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is: a) granted as to the portion of the second cause of action for negligent misrepresentation and the third cause of action for breach of fiduciary duties, and b) denied as to the portion of the second cause of action for intentional misrepresentation and the request for punitive damages. Woodside did not oppose the motion as to the portion of its second cause of action for negligent misrepresentation and its third cause of action for breach of fiduciary duties. Defendants did not satisfy their summary judgment motion burden as to the intentional misrepresentation claim and the request for punitive damages. Defendants did not sufficiently address all of Woodside's fraud theories or recoveries. In particular defendants did not show that Woodside could not prove its allegation that defendants misrepresented their skills to induce Woodside to incur attorney's fees, which Woodside incurred. Because defendants did not show that they are able to defeat the entirety of the intentional misrepresentation claim, they are not entitled to summary adjudication on that claim per CCP 437c(f)(1). Similarly, defendants have not shown that Woodside is unable to obtain punitive damages on its intentional misrepresentation claim.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (302/HK)
